title: Grammar
description: Structured Japanese grammar patterns. This is the project's largest original contribution — there is no authoritative open grammar dataset upstream, so this file is hand-curated from general, non-copyrightable facts about Japanese grammar. Explicitly NOT derived from Tae Kim's Guide (CC-BY-NC-SA, license incompatible with our CC-BY-SA 4.0 output), nor from any other copyrighted grammar reference. See the authorship_statement in data/grammar/grammar.json metadata for full provenance details. Every entry carries a review_status field tracking whether it has been reviewed by the community or by a native speaker.

metadata.description: Build metadata: source provenance, license, generation date, counts, field documentation, and attribution.

grammar_points.description: Array of curated grammar point entries.

review_coverage.description: Summary of how many entries are at each review status.

by_level.description: Count of grammar points per JLPT level.

curation_outliers.description: Statistics on entries with sparse examples, missing related, etc.

tatoeba_linkage.description: Statistics on Tatoeba example sentence linkage rates.

id.description: Stable project-assigned identifier for this grammar point (slug-form, e.g., 'te-form-kudasai').

pattern.description: The grammar pattern in Japanese notation (e.g., '～ください', '～たい', 'AはBよりC').

pattern_alt.description: Alternative spellings or notations of the same pattern.

level.description: Community-consensus JLPT level, sourced primarily from Waller. Not JLPT-official.

meaning_en.description: Concise English explanation of what this pattern means or does.

meaning_detailed.description: Longer explanation covering nuance, usage notes, and contrasts with related patterns. Written in this project's own words.

formation.description: How to form this pattern (e.g., 'Verb-て-form + ください', 'い-adjective stem + く + ありません').

formation_notes.description: Additional notes on formation, including irregular cases and exceptions.

related.description: IDs of related grammar points (synonymous, contrasting, or building blocks).

sentence_id.description: Sentence ID in '{source}-{n}' format (e.g., 'tatoeba-12345') if this example is linked to a corpus sentence.

source.description: Where this example came from: 'tatoeba', 'original' (written for this project), 'waller', etc.

review_status.description: Current review state of this entry. New entries start at 'draft'.

sources.description: Explicit list of references consulted when writing this entry (URLs, book citations, etc.).

tatoeba_pattern_matches.description: Sentence IDs (in '{source}-{n}' format, e.g., 'tatoeba-74694', 'kftt-1') where the grammar pattern appears in the sentence text. Found via pattern-string search, not example-text matching. Up to 5 per grammar point.
